sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the layout clearly:
Left panel (cols 8-23):
- Top row of bars: B, B, B, B (all black)
- Rows 2-5: all w (gray)
- Below: two small panels with yellow shapes
Right panel (cols 39-54):
- Top row of bars: w, w, w, w (all gray)
- Rows 2-5: all w (gray)
- Above: checkerboard with two yellow shapes
- Blue circle at bottom
The left panel seems to be a "reference" or "target" configuration. The right panel is what I need to configure.
In Level 1, the solution was to set all bars to B. Here, the left panel shows B,B,B,B in the top row, and the right panel shows w,w,w,w.
Maybe I need to make the right panel's bars match the left panel's bars? So I need to set the right panel's top row to B,B,B,B.
But wait, there are 5 rows of bars in each panel. The left panel has:
- Row 1: B, B, B, B
- Rows 2-5: w, w, w, w
The right panel has:
- Row 1: w, w, w, w
- Rows 2-5: w, w, w, w
So I need to set the right panel's top row to B,B,B,B to match the left panel.
Let me also look at the small panels at the bottom left. There's a blue panel with yellow shapes and a dark panel with a yellow shape. These might represent the target shapes or something else.
Let me try setting the right panel's top row to B and clicking the blue circle.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Level 2 has two panels. Left panel (reference) has top row B,B,B,B. Right panel has all w bars. Two yellow shapes on right checkerboard.
Goal model: Make right panel bars match left panel bars (set top row to B,B,B,B).
Plan: Set right panel top row (row 33, cols 39,44,49,54) to B and click blue circle.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Set right panel top row to B
actions = []
for col in [39, 44, 49, 54]:
    actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': col})

action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
